topPlaces.name: Dilkusha Kothi
topPlaces.description: Dilkusha Kothi is the remains of an eighteenth-century house built in the English baroque style in the quiet Dilkusha area of Lucknow in India. Today there are only a few towers and external walls as a monument, though the extensive gardens remain.

topPlaces.name: Loreto Convent Lucknow
topPlaces.description: Loreto Convent, Lucknow is an educational institution founded in 1872 and located in Lucknow, the capital of Uttar Pradesh, India. Loreto Convent is under the management of the Sisters of the Institute of the Blessed Virgin Mary, usually known as the Loreto Sisters.

topPlaces.name: Bal Vidya Mandir, Lucknow
topPlaces.description: Bal Vidya Mandir (aka BVM) is an English medium, co-educational Senior Secondary Residential/Day School located in Lucknow, India. It was founded in 1963 by then Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh, Chandra Bhanu Gupta, The school is fully affiliated to CBSE, imparts education from Nursery to Class XII (10+2).

topPlaces.name: 5, Kalidas Marg
topPlaces.description: 5, Kalidas Marg is the official residence of the Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh located at Kalidas Marg in Lucknow, India. It occupies a large area along with the bungalow.

topPlaces.name: Bibiyapur Kothi
topPlaces.description: Bibiyapur Kothi is a historic building in Lucknow, India. Built by Asaf-ud-Daula, Nawab of Awadh, the residence is now administered by the Indian Army.

topPlaces.name: Department of Infrastructure and Industrial Development
topPlaces.description: The Department of Infrastructure and Industrial Development, also known as Infrastructure and Industrial Development Department (IIDD) is a department of the Government of Uttar Pradesh. The department was established to create an enabling environment for industrial growth in Uttar Pradesh.

image.alt: Department of Infrastructure and Industrial Development

topPlaces.name: Martin Purwa
topPlaces.description: Martin Purwa, also known as Martinpurwa or Martinpura) is a neighbourhood of the city of Lucknow in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. It is part of the Vikramaditya ward.

topPlaces.name: Jan Bhavan, Lucknow
topPlaces.description: Jan Bhavan, formerly Raj Bhavan (translation: Governor's House) is the official residence of the governor of Uttar Pradesh. It is located in the capital city of Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit All Saints Garrison Church, Lucknow?
faq.a: The best time to visit All Saints Garrison Church, Lucknow is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach All Saints Garrison Church, Lucknow?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chaudhary Charan Singh International Airport, Lucknow (9 km) and the nearest railway station is Lucknow Charbagh (4 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for All Saints Garrison Church, Lucknow?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of All Saints Garrison Church, Lucknow at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in All Saints Garrison Church, Lucknow?
faq.a: Stays in All Saints Garrison Church, Lucknow start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in All Saints Garrison Church, Lucknow?
faq.a: The top attractions are Dilkusha Kothi, Loreto Convent Lucknow, Bal Vidya Mandir, Lucknow, 5, Kalidas Marg, Bibiyapur Kothi.
